disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Afebrile, well, not toxic; rash confined to the legs and not rapidly spreading to the trunk (against meningococcaemia)
trigger_question_examples: Do you have a fever, rigors, or feel very unwell?
Is the rash spreading quickly onto your trunk, or staying on the legs?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever or rigors, I feel okay in myself, and it's staying on my shins.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No abdominal pain, no blood in urine/cola-coloured urine, no joint pain (no HSP organ involvement so far)
trigger_question_examples: Any tummy pain, blood in your urine, or dark/cola-coloured urine?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No tummy pain, my urine looks normal, and no joint pain.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symmetric palpable purpura on dependent (lower-leg) areas following a recent upper respiratory infection; no anticoagulant use
trigger_question_examples: Are the spots slightly raised/palpable and symmetric on both legs?
Did anything precede this, like a cold, and are you on any blood thinners?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They're slightly raised and on both shins evenly; I had a cold two weeks ago and I'm not on any blood thinners.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: It's not the meningitis rash then — can I just stay home and rest?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Reassure that the well, afebrile, leg-confined picture is against meningococcaemia, but explain that a non-blanching (purpuric) rash still needs same-day in-person assessment to check platelets and kidneys (urine/BP), because those cannot be done over video — while giving a clear meningococcal and abdominal/renal safety-net.
